title: SuggestionResolved payload
description: Emitted when a suggestion closes via suggestion_resolve. Kind-discriminated: the `kind` field names the resolution. The three kinds — adopted / deferred / rejected — are the suggestion-side analogue of bug's fixed/wontfix/etc. and are deliberately distinct vocabulary. NOTE: `adopted` + `routed_chain_slug` + `resolved_commit_sha` is the canonical 'this shipped' shape; there is no separate `implemented` kind. For kind=adopted with a routing pointer, the routed task is also recorded in envelope refs.related_entities so projection folds can join without re-parsing the payload.

kind.description: Resolution kind. `adopted` — the suggestion's proposal is accepted (may also carry routed_chain_slug / routed_task_slug / routed_bug_slug naming the work that landed or is queued). `deferred` — agreed in principle but not now (resolution_note typically carries the revisit signal). `rejected` — the proposal is declined.

commit_sha.description: For kind=adopted that's already shipped: the commit that landed the change. Accepts the literal sentinel 'unversioned' for artifacts outside any git repo. May be null when adoption queues work in a chain that hasn't shipped yet — stamp later via suggestion_stamp_sha.

routed_chain_slug.description: For kind=adopted: the chain absorbing the proposal's work. null for other kinds.

routed_task_slug.description: For kind=adopted: the specific task in routed_chain_slug that executes the work. null for other kinds.

routed_bug_slug.description: For kind=adopted: if the adoption uncovers a concrete fix tracked as a bug, the bug slug. Symmetric counterpart to bug.routed_suggestion_slug — bidirectional bug↔suggestion routing per chain `agent-suggestion-box` design_decisions §1.

resolution_note.description: Free-text note from the resolver. For kind=adopted: what shipped (or the chain+task that will ship it). For kind=deferred: why-not-now + revisit signal. For kind=rejected: reasoning. Distinct from envelope.rationale (required for agent actors, answers 'why this resolution'); resolution_note is operational context for downstream readers.
